summary: Added GALE01 configuration to block known invalid or spurious relocations so shiftability analysis is not polluted by data that only looks like relocatable pointers. The PR also merged two adjacent data symbols by expanding hsd_8040FF80 and removing lbl_80410000, while preserving the following jumptable boundary at .data:0x80430B40. Evidence is limited to the title, diff, and an automated decomp.dev report showing no output changes.

change_classification.primary_type: configuration metadata fix for relocation analysis

what_changed: Introduced a block_relocations section in config/GALE01/config.yml.
Blocked relocation sources for GXColors in dbitem at 0x803EAE08 and 0x804DBCF0.
Blocked relocation sources for bitmasks in psdisptev at .text:0x803A3A60, .text:0x803A3A68, .text:0x803A3AE8, and .text:0x803A3AF8.
Blocked a range described as a raw texture array that creates spurious relocs, from .data:0x8040CD40 through end .data:0x80430B40.
In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, removed lbl_80410000 at .data:0x80410000 and expanded hsd_8040FF80 from size 0x80 to size 0x20BC0.
Left jumptable_80430B40 as the next symbol after the expanded hsd_8040FF80 region.
smart_moves: Used block_relocations instead of forcing fake symbols or splitting raw data around every pointer-like value.
Grouped blocked relocations with comments that explain the suspected data category: GXColors, bitmasks, and raw texture array.
Used section-qualified addresses for text and data entries where appropriate, making the relocation-block intent unambiguous.
Blocked the raw texture array as a range ending exactly at .data:0x80430B40, avoiding accidental absorption of the following jumptable symbol.
Consolidated an artificial-looking lbl_80410000 split into hsd_8040FF80, reducing symbol noise caused by spurious relocation discovery.
decomp_lessons: Shiftability metadata may need explicit relocation suppression for constants, colors, bitmasks, or raw texture bytes that coincidentally look like addresses.
When a data blob produces false relocation hits, prefer documenting and blocking the invalid source range over creating misleading labels.
A symbol boundary immediately before a known jumptable should be preserved carefully; here the data range ends at .data:0x80430B40 and jumptable_80430B40 remains separate.
Config-level relocation blocks can be a low-risk fix when the generated report shows no binary or analysis output changes.
Evidence for why these specific addresses are invalid comes only from inline comments in the diff; future work should retain such comments or add more context when adding relocation blocks.
naming_conventions: Address-derived placeholder names are retained for unresolved data, such as hsd_8040FF80, lbl_80430B84, and lbl_80430BD0.
Known-library-looking global data continues to use HSD_SisLib_8040C680, HSD_SisLib_8040CB00, and HSD_SisLib_8040CD40 style names.
Jumptable symbols use the jumptable_<address> convention, as with jumptable_80430B40.
Temporary generic lbl_<address> symbols may be removed when later evidence shows they are merely an artificial split inside a larger raw data region.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use config.yml block_relocations entries to stop invalid relocation candidates from affecting shiftability.
Block individual source addresses for isolated false positives.
Use a source plus end pair for contiguous false-positive data ranges.
Annotate relocation blocks by data interpretation, such as GXColors, bitmasks, or raw texture arrays, to make future validation easier.
After blocking spurious relocs in a raw data range, update symbols.txt so the object sizing matches the intended contiguous region.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The automated decomp.dev comment reported: 'Report for GALE01 (560ac61 - 0e99c07): No changes.'
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that add block_relocations to config/GALE01/config.yml.
Search for spurious relocation fixes involving raw texture arrays or color constants.
Look for later changes touching hsd_8040FF80, HSD_SisLib_8040CD40, or jumptable_80430B40.
Audit whether GXColors in dbitem and bitmasks in psdisptev later received stronger symbol/type documentation.
